claimText: Keibul Lamjao National Park is the only floating national park in the world.
evidenceExcerpt: The only floating national park in the world.
examUse: Statement-based question on unique geographical features of Indian National Parks.
distractorHooks: It is the largest but not the only floating park

claimText: Loktak Lake, where Keibul Lamjao National Park is located, is recognized as both a Ramsar site and a Montreux Record site.
evidenceExcerpt: Located in Loktak Lake, which is both a Ramsar site and a Montreux Record site.
examUse: Statement-based question on wetland conservation status.
distractorHooks: It was removed from the Montreux Record
It is a Ramsar site but not on the Montreux Record

claimText: Phumdis are floating islands of vegetation characteristic of Loktak Lake and Keibul Lamjao National Park.
evidenceExcerpt: Characterized by Phumdis, which are floating islands of vegetation.
distractorHooks: Phumdis are a type of mangrove vegetation
Phumdis are found in the backwaters of Kerala

claimText: Keibul Lamjao National Park is the primary habitat for the Sangai, also known as the Brow-antlered deer.
evidenceExcerpt: Primary habitat for the Brow-antlered deer, also known as Sangai.
distractorHooks: Primary habitat for the Hangul (Kashmir stag)
Primary habitat for the Swamp deer (Barasingha)
